c74object.name: tap.ladder~
c74object.description: The tap.ladder~ object is a virtual-analog model of the classic 4-pole transistor-ladder filter: a zero-delay-feedback ladder with tanh saturation in every stage, running oversampled (2x by default). The prewarped tuning stays accurate into the top octaves at any sample rate. Resonance reaches clean self-oscillation (1.0 is the edge; above it the filter sings at the cutoff frequency, amplitude-limited by the saturation — give it a ping to start, as a perfectly silent filter is a fixed point). The drive attribute pushes the input into the nonlinearity for anything from subtle thickening to full growl, and comp restores the passband level that a real ladder loses as resonance rises. A mode attribute selects lowpass, bandpass, and highpass responses by mixing the ladder's stage taps (the Oberheim Xpander technique). A signal in the right inlet modulates the cutoff with true per-sample resolution. All parameters glide smoothly, and sixteen preset slots can be stored and morphed between. For the simple linear Stilson/Smith ladder, see tap.fourpole~ — this object is its nonlinear sibling. Note that exact unity gain is impossible in a saturating filter: large signals are gently compressed by design. Single-channel: wrap in mc. for multichannel.

method.name: float
method.description: In the right inlet, sets the cutoff frequency in Hz.

method.name: store
method.description: Store the current parameters in a preset slot (1-16).

method.name: recall
method.description: Morph all parameters to a stored preset (1-16). The optional second argument overrides the morph time in milliseconds (default: the interp attribute).

method.name: clear
method.description: Reset the filter state (this also stops self-oscillation).

attribute.name: frequency
attribute.description: Cutoff frequency in Hz (10-20000). A signal in the right inlet overrides this with per-sample resolution.

attribute.name: resonance
attribute.description: Resonance (0-1.1). 1.0 is the edge of self-oscillation; above it the filter oscillates at the cutoff, bounded by the tanh stages.

attribute.name: drive
attribute.description: Input drive in dB (-24..24): how hard the signal hits the saturating ladder stages.

attribute.name: comp
attribute.description: Passband-loss compensation (0-1). 0 is the authentic ladder passband droop at high resonance; 1 fully compensates.

attribute.name: mode
attribute.description: Filter response via pole mixing: 0 = lp24, 1 = lp12, 2 = bp12, 3 = bp24, 4 = hp12, 5 = hp24.

attribute.name: oversample
attribute.description: Internal oversampling factor (1, 2, or 4; default 2). Higher factors reduce aliasing from the saturation, at higher CPU cost.

attribute.name: interp
attribute.description: Default preset-morph time in milliseconds used by the recall message.

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Anti-zipper interpolation time in milliseconds applied to parameter changes.
